articles.title: The Political Backlash to Prediction Markets Has Arrived
articles.summary: Politicians and regulators are pushing back against the growing influence of prediction markets in politics and policy. This resistance could trigger new restrictions or oversight that limits market access and reduces the betting opportunities that have made political prediction markets so profitable.

articles.title: Lawmakers Press to Limit Prediction Bets by Policymakers
articles.summary: Congress is pushing to ban or restrict government officials from betting on political outcomes and policy decisions. The move could legitimize prediction markets by addressing the insider trading concerns that regulators have used to justify heavy restrictions on political betting.

articles.title: We Haven’t Seen the Worst of What Gambling and Prediction Markets Will Do to America
articles.summary: A new piece argues that gambling and prediction markets will cause more social damage than we've witnessed so far. If regulators buy this narrative, expect stricter oversight that could kneecap the prediction market boom before it really gets started.

articles.title: Media’s New Ethical Dilemma: Polymarket and Kalshi
articles.summary: Newsrooms are wrestling with whether to cover prediction market odds as legitimate polling data or gambling content that deserves different editorial standards. The coverage decisions will shape whether platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi get treated as serious forecasting tools or relegated to the sports betting section, directly impacting their mainstream credibility and user growth.

articles.title: Opinion | Trump is right about polls — and the better bet
articles.summary: Trump's calling out polling accuracy again, and this time someone thinks he's onto something about systematic polling errors or methodology flaws. If polls are consistently missing the mark, smart money will keep flowing toward betting markets as the more reliable gauge of actual electoral sentiment.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Changing the Music Industry
articles.summary: Music labels and streaming platforms are starting to use prediction markets to forecast which songs will hit, which artists will break through, and how album releases will perform. Smart money is realizing that fan sentiment and crowd wisdom often beat A&R executives at spotting the next big thing, creating new opportunities for bettors who understand music trends better than industry insiders.

articles.title: Kalshi and Polymarket using influencers to lure in a group that isn’t betting: Women
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms Kalshi and Polymarket are launching influencer marketing campaigns specifically targeting women, a demographic that has largely stayed away from political betting. This push could dramatically expand the market size if successful, since women represent roughly half the population but a tiny fraction of current prediction market users.

articles.title: An Evening at Polymarket’s Totally Broken Pop-up Bar
articles.summary: Polymarket hosted a pop-up bar event that apparently went sideways, likely featuring technical glitches, payment issues, or other operational failures that left attendees frustrated. When the world's biggest prediction market can't run a simple bar without breaking, it raises questions about whether they can handle the infrastructure demands of processing billions in election bets.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A trader netted nearly $1M on Polymarket by correctly predicting Iran-related events with unusual precision. This signals either exceptional geopolitical insight or potential information advantages that could reshape how markets price Middle Eastern risk.

articles.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Founders Back New Prediction Market VC Fund
articles.summary: Kalshi's Tarek Mansour and Polymarket's Shayne Coplan are backing a new VC fund focused on prediction market investments. This signals that the biggest players in the space see massive growth ahead and want to fund the next wave of betting platforms before regulators figure out how to kill them.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.
